title: Search Suggestions System
problem_statement: Build a search suggestion system that returns up to three lexicographically smallest product names that match each prefix of the search word.

examples.explanation: At each typed prefix, return up to three matching products in sorted order.

constraints: All strings are lowercase English letters
intuition: Sort products lexicographically and use binary search to find the first match for each prefix, then collect up to three candidates.

approaches.name: Sorted Products and Binary Search
approaches.description: Sort the list of products once, then for each prefix find the lower bound and scan the next three products to form the suggestion list.
approaches.time: O(n log n + m log n + 3m)
approaches.why_not: A trie is also possible but sorting plus binary search is simpler and fast enough for the given constraints.

interview_qa.a: Sorting ensures the matching suggestions are already in lexicographic order so only the first three candidates are needed for each prefix.

interview_qa.q: How do you find matches quickly?
interview_qa.a: Use binary search on the sorted list to locate the first product whose prefix matches the current query prefix.
